common.noUploadNotice: This tool parses everything on your machine. No code, conversations, or diagnostics are uploaded.
common.mustRestartAgent: You must restart your AI agent (Claude Code / Cursor / Codex, etc.); otherwise the hook will not take effect and Diagnostic will still flag errors.
common.mustReopenTerminal: After installing on Windows, close and reopen every terminal (PowerShell / CMD / Windows Terminal / IDE built-in terminals) so the updated PATH takes effect.
common.winAdminHint: Reopen git-ai-studio or the terminal as Administrator, then try again.
common.winCrlfHint: This repository has no `* text=auto eol=lf` declaration. On Windows, CRLF can shift line authorship by 1 line; consider adding that line at the repository root.
common.winPathSafeHint: Avoid spaces and non-ASCII characters in the install path. If GIT_AI_HOME currently points to a directory with spaces or Chinese characters, switch it to a short pure-ASCII path (e.g. C:\git-ai) and reinstall.
common.ccSwitchWarning: When using cc-switch to switch models, make sure the hook in settings is still there — cc-switch may overwrite the hooks block in ~/.claude/settings.json, so confirm on the Hooks page after every switch.

sideEffects: The install script writes the git-ai binary to ~/.git-ai/bin/
The install script registers the git proxy shim at the top of PATH
If an AI agent is installed, writes its corresponding config (~/.claude/settings.json, etc.)
uninstallRemoved: Removes the ~/.git-ai/ directory (binary, config.json, cached working_logs)
Runs git-ai remove-hooks to clean up registered AI agent hooks
uninstallKept: AI authorship notes under refs/notes/ai in each repository are kept and remain readable after reinstall
Historical .git/ai/working_logs/ entries in each repository are kept
AI agent config files themselves are not removed

mode.official: Official hooks (git-ai install): cross-platform, automatically registered for Claude/Cursor. Recommended.
mode.none: Not configured: AI agent edits will not be tagged and will all fall into unknown_additions. Metrics will be skewed.

toOfficial: Runs git-ai install, writing type=command hooks into ~/.claude/settings.json and similar files
Existing non-git-ai entries (cc-switch / user-defined hooks) are left untouched
toNone: Removes every git-ai-owned hook entry from ~/.claude/settings.json
Keeps cc-switch and user-registered hooks untouched
All AI-authored lines from then on will be counted as unknown_additions; metrics will be skewed

staleLock.title: Stale git-ai daemon lock
staleLock.hint: The previous daemon process exited abnormally without releasing daemon.lock. Every git-ai client command (including hook-triggered checkpoints) is now permanently blocked with "daemon startup blocked: lock held at ...".
staleLock.stepLabel: Action: delete the two files below and retry the hook — the next git-ai command will spin up a fresh daemon automatically.

blockedLock.title: git-ai daemon lock is still held
blockedLock.hint: daemon.lock is still held by git-ai.exe, but daemon.pid.json is missing or unusable. Deleting the lock directly will fail and every git-ai client command will keep reporting "daemon startup blocked: lock held at ...".
blockedLock.stepLabel: Action: kill the git-ai.exe holding the lock first, then delete daemon.lock / daemon.pid.json, then retry your git-ai command.

stats.workingScopeHint: Note: this is the current repo's uncommitted changes; the Dashboard's "Uncommitted" card aggregates your selected repo set (which may not include this repo).

repoMissing.title: Pick a repository first
repoMissing.description: Stats needs a specific git repository to compute per-commit AI authorship. Choose one or scan to add on the Repositories page.

gitAiMissing.title: git-ai not detected
gitAiMissing.description: The git-ai binary is missing, so stats cannot be generated. Come back here after installing.

noHead.title: Repository has no commits yet
noHead.description: The repository's HEAD is unreadable (empty repo / freshly initialized). Come back after the first commit lands.

noteText.merge: This commit is a merge commit. Merge authorship logs are allowed to be empty, so the AI line count is typically 0.
noteText.emptyAdditions: This commit added no lines (pure delete / rename / merge). All buckets are 0 and shares display as —.
noteText.workingLogsMissing: This commit added lines, but all of them fall into "unknown", and history shows no AI lines either — meaning the AI agent did not trigger git-ai's checkpoint hook. Confirm on the Hooks page.

cacheHint.cachePolicy: Stats on this page are considered stale after 30s automatically; switching commits / repositories or clicking Refresh re-fetches. git-ai stats --json runs as a local subprocess and uploads nothing.

filter.noUserEmail: git user.email not configured; "Only mine" filter is unavailable

summary.truncatedTitle: Older commits may not be included

detail.emptyHint: Select a commit to view attribution details
detail.failedNotice: Collecting git-ai stats for this commit failed; the numbers below are 0-bucket placeholders, not real data. Refresh or check Diagnostic.

stats.footer: Parsed locally, never uploaded · Cached {{seconds}}s · Per-file shows only real AI lines (no fabricated denominator)

commitList.workingHint: Local working tree, not yet committed

metricSources.headAiRate: AI lines / total added lines (current HEAD)
metricSources.windowAiTotal: Sum of AI lines across every commit in the window
metricSources.hookCoverage: Share of commits that carry a git-ai authorship record

metricErrors.headFailed: Failed to read stats for current HEAD
metricErrors.headUnavailable: Stats for current HEAD are unavailable

repoMissing.title: Pick a repository first
repoMissing.description: Dashboard needs a git repository to aggregate historical stats. Choose one on the Repositories page.

gitAiMissing.title: git-ai not detected
gitAiMissing.description: Dashboard's historical aggregation depends on git-ai stats. Come back here after installing.

noReposSelected.title: No repositories selected
noReposSelected.description: The Dashboard shows your selected "aggregate set" (cross-repo overview); Commit attribution / People / Blame show the single repo you're currently drilling into. Add at least one repo on the Repositories page, then come back for the cross-repo trend.

dashboard.dataHint: All parsed locally by git-ai. Window AI share = Σ AI lines / Σ (human + unknown + AI) lines; click the ⓘ next to any metric / section title for the full formula and definition.
dashboard.currentNotIncluded: The repo you're drilling into, "{{repo}}", isn't in the Dashboard's aggregate set — the numbers above come from your other selected repos, not it.
dashboard.failedReposHint: {{n}} repos failed to collect and are excluded from the aggregate (never counted as zero).
dashboard.truncatedReposHint: {{n}} repos hit the 500-commit cap; older commits are not included in this window.

emptyWindow.title: No commits in the selected range
emptyWindow.descriptionTemplate: This repository has no commits within "{{rangeLabel}}". Try a wider window.

dashboard.chartAllZero: Every commit in the window added no lines (pure merge / pure delete / pure rename). There is no time-series data to visualize.
dashboard.truncatedHint: The repository commit count exceeded the {{cap}}-commit cap; older commits in this window may have been skipped. Use a shorter window, or wait for pagination support in P10.
dashboard.failedHint: {{n}} commits failed stats collection this run. They are excluded from "production" and the time series so the numbers aren't polluted by zero-bucket fallbacks.

repoMissing.title: Pick a repository first
repoMissing.description: Blame needs a git repository to look up per-line AI authorship.

gitAiMissing.title: git-ai not detected
gitAiMissing.description: The Blame view depends on git-ai blame. Come back here after installing.

noHead.title: Repository has no commits yet
noHead.description: HEAD is unreadable; come back after the first commit lands.

commitNotFound.title: Commit not found
commitNotFound.descriptionTemplate: git cannot find {{sha}} — it may have been rewritten by rebase. Pick another commit or return to HEAD.

fileNotInHead.title: This file does not exist in that commit
fileNotInHead.descriptionTemplate: {{file}} is not present in this commit (it may be working-tree only or added later).

fileTooLarge.title: File too large
fileTooLarge.descriptionTemplate: The file ({{sizeKb}} KB) exceeds the {{limitKb}} KB limit. Blame skips rendering to avoid IPC stalls.

fileBinary.title: Binary file
fileBinary.description: The first 8000 bytes contain a NUL; treated as binary and not rendered.

popover.loginRequired: Not logged in to git-ai; cannot resolve the human author
popover.scopeWarningRepoWide: (per-prompt counts from this note, not just this file; summed across squashed/merged commits)
popover.driftCaveat: Line content comes from the latest commit; the prompt was generated by the AI back then and **may have been edited by humans afterwards**, so the two need not match.
popover.mergeCaveat: If a line's last-touched commit is a merge, its authorship log may be empty (spec v3.0.0 §2.2); the UI shows it as a non-AI gray line.

refPicker.refNotFoundTemplate: git cannot find {{r}} (or it is not a commit object). Check the spelling or pick another commit.

timeRange.customInvalidRange: Start date must be on or before end date
timeRange.customTooWideTemplate: Custom range cannot span more than {{max}} days

showRaw.dialogDescription: The raw text from upstream git-ai show (JSON metadata + attestations). When asking for help, copying this is the safest thing to share.
showRaw.empty: This commit has no AI authorship data (no authorship data).
showRaw.loadFailed: Failed to load raw git-ai show
showRaw.degradedRepoMissing: No repository selected — pick one on the Repo page first.
showRaw.degradedGitAiMissing: git-ai is not installed — install it on the Install page first.

effectiveIgnore.title: Active ignore patterns
effectiveIgnore.hint: File path patterns git-ai actually skips when computing stats / blame. Sources: built-in defaults + the repository's .gitattributes (linguist-generated) + .git-ai-ignore at the repository root.
effectiveIgnore.listEmpty: No patterns are active (rare; usually means an abnormal git-ai version or that the repository has no .git-ai-ignore and the defaults are disabled).
effectiveIgnore.degradedRepoMissing: No repository selected — pick one on the Repo page first, then come back.
effectiveIgnore.degradedGitAiMissing: git-ai is not installed — install it on the Install page first.

repoMissing.title: Pick a repository first
repoMissing.description: The Notes page needs a git repository to list refs/notes/ai.

noNotesInRepo.title: This repository has no AI authorship yet
noNotesInRepo.description: git notes --ref=ai is empty. There may be no commits with a checkpoint yet, or hooks are disabled. Confirm on the Hooks page.

commitNoNote.title: No AI authorship for this commit
commitNoNote.description: git notes --ref=ai show <sha> failed. Either the commit never had a checkpoint, or the note was removed by git-ai rewrite-authorship.

actions.blameDisabledNonHead: Line attribution is available only for HEAD-commit notes; switch to the HEAD commit's notes to enable it.
actions.openLineAttrMultiRangeTemplate: View line attribution ({{n}} ranges; deep link uses the first L{{range}})
actions.lineRangeUnparseable: line_ranges could not be parsed; line jump disabled
actions.blameDisabledDriftCaveat: Line numbers are based on HEAD and may drift from historical commit line numbers.

instructions.title: Select a commit on the left to view its AI authorship
instructions.description: The list only shows commits that already have git notes --ref=ai. Selecting one expands attestations, prompts, humans, and sessions on the right.

parseFailed.title: Failed to parse notes
parseFailed.description: git notes show returned an unexpected format. Either git-ai schema drifted or the note content is corrupt. Expand below to see the raw error.

lowAiShare.settingsHint: When enabled, a reminder pops up if your AI share in the last 7 days drops below the threshold. By default it filters by the current repository's git user.email; if no email is available it falls back to the whole repo. Self-checks every 15 minutes; does not bother you when total added lines < 50 in a fresh repo. Disabled by default.
rules: The window is fixed at the last 7 days; the formula is AI additions / (human + unknown + AI additions).
When target emails is empty, defaults to `git config user.email` of the current repository and counts only that author; if unreadable, falls back to the whole repo.
Triggers only when total additions in the window are ≥ 50 lines AND the AI share is strictly below the threshold.
Suppressed for 5 minutes after a repository switch; afterwards it re-checks and re-reminds at the configured remind interval.
After the user clicks X or the snooze button, reminders pause for the configured snooze duration.
OS notifications fire in parallel with the in-app toast (macOS Notification Center / Linux libnotify / Windows toast); if a cooldown above gates the toast, the OS notification is suppressed too.
lowAiShare.targetEmailsPlaceholder:
Empty = current repo's git user.email
alice@example.com
bob@example.com
lowAiShare.targetEmailsHelp: Separate multiple emails with commas or newlines. For multi-author repos, name yourself or the lead; when empty, the current repository's git user.email is read automatically.
lowAiShare.toastTitleWithRepoTemplate: AI share in the last 7 days is {{pct}}% (below {{threshold}}% threshold) · {{repoName}}
lowAiShare.toastTitleTemplate: AI share in the last 7 days is {{pct}}% (below {{threshold}}% threshold)
lowAiShare.toastDescription: You can disable this reminder permanently in Settings.
lowAiShare.dismissedToastTemplate: Dismissed. No reminders for {{duration}}. You can disable this permanently in Settings.

repoMissing.title: Pick a repository first
repoMissing.description: Checkpoints needs a git repository to read working_logs.

noHead.title: Repository has no commits yet
noHead.description: The checkpoint directory is keyed by HEAD sha; no base is available for an empty repo.

gitAiMissing.title: git-ai not detected
gitAiMissing.description: mock_checkpoint invokes the git-ai CLI; install it first. Reading the list itself does not require it, but the mock entry point will be disabled.

workingLogsDirMissing.title: No working_logs directory for this HEAD yet
workingLogsDirMissing.description: .git/ai/working_logs/<HEAD>/ does not exist, which usually means git-ai has not run a checkpoint at this HEAD yet. Confirm the AI agent's hook is registered on the Hooks page, or click "Debug" in the top right to generate test data via the mock entry.

header.subtitle: Working-directory view: AI / human edit checkpoints before the next commit (reads .git/ai/working_logs/<HEAD>/checkpoints.jsonl)
header.preCommitNote: Uncommitted: the next commit will write these authorship entries into git notes, viewable on the Notes page.

human.label: Human
human.tooltip: Untracked human edit (legacy / no IDE extension reporting)

aiAgent.label: AI Agent
aiAgent.tooltip: AI agent mode: bulk generation by Claude Code / Cursor Composer / Cline / etc.

aiTab.label: AI Completion
aiTab.tooltip: AI inline completion: GitHub Copilot / Cursor Tab

knownHuman.label: Known Human
knownHuman.tooltip: The IDE extension explicitly reported a human save event

card.agentMetadataLabel: agent_metadata (may include IDE / session id, collapsed by default)
card.blameAtHeadCaveat: Blame shows authorship for the HEAD commit, which may differ from this checkpoint's working-dir content at the time.
card.noEntries: This checkpoint has no file entries

emptyList.title: No checkpoints at current HEAD
emptyList.description: Hooks write to .git/ai/working_logs/<HEAD>/checkpoints.jsonl when an AI agent or human edits. If the file is missing or empty, the hook may not be configured (go to Hooks) or no edits have happened yet.

mockDialog.intro: This action really writes to .git/ai/working_logs/<HEAD>/checkpoints.jsonl, for debugging.
sideEffects: Appends a Checkpoint (kind determined by preset) to .git/ai/working_logs/<HEAD_SHA>/checkpoints.jsonl
Affected files: the pathspecs you enter; if left empty, git-ai tags all currently dirty files
The checkpoint is counted by git-ai stats / status and will land in git notes --ref=ai on the next commit
Immediately visible in Stats / Dashboard / Blame / Notes (queries auto-invalidate, numbers change)
**Irreversible**: git-ai has no `checkpoint reset` CLI; delete .git/ai/working_logs/<HEAD_SHA>/checkpoints.jsonl or the whole sha directory by hand if needed
mockDialog.pathspecsLabel: pathspecs (one per line; empty = all dirty files)
mockDialog.pathspecsHelp: Relative to repo root; supports git pathspec syntax. When empty, uses the actual dirty list from git status.
mockDialog.dirtyPreviewTitle: Current dirty file preview (an empty pathspec will affect these files):
mockDialog.dirtyPreviewUnavailable: Could not read the dirty file list (the git process may be unhealthy); on run it will match the actual git status result.
mockDialog.dirtyPreviewTooManyWarnTemplate: {{n}} dirty files detected; pass explicit pathspecs to avoid mis-tagging.
mockDialog.fireAndForgetHint: The git-ai CLI is fire-and-forget: after the process exits, the daemon keeps writing asynchronously in the background. The list auto-refreshes about 800ms after this dialog closes; come back to this page later to see the new checkpoint.
mockDialog.doneOk: exit 0 — git-ai CLI has sent the checkpoint request
mockDialog.irreversibleWarn: This action is irreversible; git-ai-studio has no undo entry.

checkpoints.mockBusy: A mock task is already running
checkpoints.installBusy: An install / uninstall task is in progress; mock is disabled
checkpoints.hooksBusy: Hook switching is in progress; mock is disabled

workingDir.label: Uncommitted
workingDir.tooltipTemplate:
Uncommitted working tree: human {{h}} · unknown {{u}} · ai {{a}}
Click for details

quickFixCatalog.sectionHint: Matched live against your local diagnostic result. Click an entry to view the fix commands you can copy to the terminal, or jump to the corresponding page.
quickFixCatalog.empty: No issues detected — everything looks normal.
quickFixCatalog.dialogIntro: Suggested fix steps below. Commands are ready to copy and run; if you already know what to do, click "Go to fix" to jump to the right page.

page.title: Author Attribution
page.subtitle: Aggregates AI authorship in the current repo window by author_email; numbers are parsed locally and uploaded nowhere.
page.identityHint: Identity key = lower-cased author_email; emails differing only in case are merged into the same row. git mailmap is not used.
page.cachePolicy: Parsed locally, uploaded nowhere; results cached for {{sec}}s.

repoMissing.title: Pick a repository first
repoMissing.description: Author attribution needs a git repository to aggregate commits by author. Choose one on the Repositories page.

gitAiMissing.title: git-ai not detected
gitAiMissing.description: Author attribution depends on git-ai stats. Come back here after installing.

emptyWindow.title: No commits in the selected range
emptyWindow.description: There are no commits in this window, so nothing to aggregate by person.

emptyMine.title: You have no commits in this window
emptyMine.description: Under the "Only me" scope, the current git user made no commits in the selected range.

cannotIdentifyMe.title: Can't determine the current git user
cannotIdentifyMe.description: This repo has no user.email configured, so "Only me" can't filter. Configure it and retry, or view all authors.

people.failedHintTemplate: {{n}} commits failed stats collection this run. They are counted in "commits" but excluded from AI/human/unknown totals so the numbers aren't polluted by zero-bucket fallbacks.
people.truncatedHintTemplate: The repository commit count exceeded the {{cap}}-commit cap; older commits in this window may have been skipped. Use a shorter window before aggregating.

rowCommits.heading: Commit list (click to jump to Stats)

changedFiles.title: Files changed in this commit
changedFiles.empty: This commit has no changed files (it may be an empty commit).
changedFiles.invalidSha: That sha does not exist in the local repository or is not a valid commit.
changedFiles.jumpBlameTitle: Open in Blame (locked to this commit)
changedFiles.viewBlameTitle: View line-by-line attribution for this file

authorsJumpBlame.noAiLinesDisabled: This author has no AI-authored lines in any commit within the window; cannot jump
authorsJumpBlame.jumpTitle: Open AI lines from this author's latest commit in Blame

agentHooks.title: AI Agent Hooks
agentHooks.fixMissingDisabledTitle: No agent needs fixing right now
agentHooks.fixMissingTitleTemplate: Will write official hooks for {{n}} agent(s)

check.gitAiBinaryImpact: No AI authorship metric can be produced (without the tagger, there's no stats / blame / notes)
check.gitAiVersionImpactWarn: This version is marked as having known issues. Local stats are unaffected, but remote aggregation (team dashboard) stats may be skewed.
check.inRepoImpactMissing: When no repo is selected or the directory has no .git, no metric is computable
check.coreHooksPathImpactWarn: A custom hooksPath may disable git-ai's proxy hooks. If you've confirmed git-ai's post-commit / prepare-commit-msg are inside that path, you can ignore this.
check.anyAgentConfiguredLabel: At least one configured AI agent detected
check.anyAgentConfiguredImpactErr: No agent has a checkpoint entry; every edit will be counted as unknown_additions
check.workingLogsImpactEmpty: There are no checkpoints under the current HEAD yet. They appear after AI writes and saves code; authorship lands in git notes after commit.
check.envLabel: Critical GIT_AI_* env vars are not abnormally overridden
check.envImpactWarn: Environment variables can cause the client to use a different binary than the one installed, leading to parse and statistics inconsistencies
check.agentConfiguredImpactErrTemplate: Code edited by {{agent}} will not trigger a checkpoint; all AI lines produced by it will be marked unknown

page.title: Logs
page.intro: Every log on this page is read and rendered on your machine. It may contain local paths, API key fragments, or git-ai subprocess error text — redact before copying or sharing.

debug.intro: Runs git-ai debug once and streams stdout/stderr live. Diagnostics is the structured view; this tab gives the raw stream (handy for copying to a teammate or filing an issue).
debug.emptyIdle: Click the button to start; output streams below.

noGitAi.title: git-ai not installed
noGitAi.detail: Install git-ai on the Install page first.

debug.failed: Run failed; see the output above.

app.title: Application log
app.hint: The default tauri-plugin-log target. A single file caps at 40KB and is overwritten once exceeded (KeepOne rotation).

empty.title: No application log yet
empty.detail: The app just started or has not emitted any log yet. Run a few actions and come back.

welcome.title: Welcome to Git AI Studio
welcome.hint: Get started in 5 steps. You can skip at any time.

gitAi.title: Check git-ai installation
gitAi.hint: This client relies on the external git-ai CLI to analyze AI attribution for each commit.

repo.title: Configure a local repository
repo.hint: Pick a local Git repository. Dashboard / Stats / Blame all operate on it.

hook.title: Check hook configuration
hook.hint: Hooks automatically write a checkpoint when an agent edits code. Without hooks, AI line counts are unavailable.

done.title: All set
done.hint: All data is analyzed locally; nothing is uploaded by default.

toast.rootRequired: Please enter a scan root directory first.
toast.skipped: Onboarding skipped. You can reopen it from Settings → General.

welcome.heading: What is Git AI Studio?
welcome.intro: A local client for the external git-ai CLI — it visualizes how many lines, which lines, and which prompt the AI produced in each commit.
welcome.featureDashboard: : overall repo AI share / time buckets
welcome.featureBlame: : line-by-line view of whether AI or a human wrote it, and which prompt generated it
welcome.featureHooks: : configure AI agent hooks and troubleshoot issues
welcome.noUpload: All data is analyzed locally; nothing is uploaded by default.

gitAi.detectedBody: Studio will invoke git-ai as a subprocess to analyze each commit. Continue to the next step.
gitAi.missingBody: Studio is a client for git-ai; without it, no AI attribution data is available. Install it from the Install page, then come back and click "Re-check" to continue.

repo.emptyHint: Click "Choose directory" above to pick a root, then click "Scan" to list the Git repositories found under it. Selecting one automatically continues to the next step.

hook.configuredBody: When an AI agent edits files, a checkpoint is written automatically, and Dashboard data is being collected normally.
hook.unconfiguredBody: AI agent file edits need a hook to be captured by git-ai. Configure it on the Hooks page (official mode is cross-platform and recommended). After changing it, you must restart your AI agent and terminal for it to take effect.

ccSwitch.label: cc-switch guard
ccSwitch.hint: Automatically restore the git-ai hook when cc-switch switches Codex / Claude profiles.

lowAiShare.label: Low AI share alert
lowAiShare.hint: Show a toast and send an OS notification when the 7-day AI share falls below the threshold (default 80%).

daemonUnhealthy.label: Daemon unhealthy alert
daemonUnhealthy.hint: Alert via the OS notification center when a daemon lock blockage takes down all hooks.

done.completeBody: You can now open the Dashboard to see AI attribution data for each commit.
done.advancedTitle: Advanced: 3 monitoring alerts (all off by default)
done.advancedHint: Enable as needed — leaving them off is fine. Data collection itself depends only on hooks, not on these alerts.
done.footerHint: To run onboarding again: Settings → General → "Reopen onboarding". Having trouble: the Diagnostic page has an automated checklist and the Logs page shows live git-ai debug output.

page.title: User manual
page.subtitle: Common git-ai commands, metric definitions, and troubleshooting. Everything is parsed locally; nothing is uploaded.

attribution.hint: Which git operations get captured as AI authorship, and whether the source branch keeps its attribution. git-ai captures git operations through a git proxy + background daemon + git trace2 + reflog (it no longer uses git's own hooks), and re-maps attribution onto the new commits on amend / rebase / squash / cherry-pick.
attribution.preconditionTitle: Precondition: all automatic attribution requires running under git-ai
attribution.precondition: git-ai no longer relies on git's built-in hooks. It installs itself as a git proxy (a shim at the front of PATH) plus a resident daemon, and recognizes what you ran via git trace2 events and the reflog. So the automatic attribution / re-mapping below only takes effect when the git operation runs on a machine where git-ai is installed and the daemon is live. On a machine without git-ai, or when the real git is invoked directly bypassing the proxy (some GUIs, CI), the operation is not captured and those lines fall into "unknown" — that is an honest representation, not a bug.

items.op: git commit after an AI agent edits
items.target: The new commit gets a refs/notes/ai note; lines the AI changed count as AI
items.original: N/A (a new commit rewrites no history; parent notes are untouched)
items.note: Requires the daemon to be live AND a checkpoint to have fired while the agent edited. Missing either, those lines don't error — they fall into "unknown" rather than AI.

items.op: Plain human commit / no checkpoint / pre-git-ai history
items.target: Not counted as AI: if a git-ai IDE extension observed real human typing → "human"; otherwise → "unknown"
items.note: git-ai attaches a note to every commit, but "who owns this line" depends on whether the edit was observed at the time.

items.target: The rewritten commit inherits the original's AI line attribution (line numbers recomputed against new content); human contributors and sessions are preserved
items.original: The original commit becomes dangling; its note is neither copied to the new commit nor actively deleted — it disappears with git garbage collection
items.note: Requires a live daemon; a plain git amend outside git-ai gets no automatic attribution.

items.target: New commits are attributed automatically: untouched lines shift line numbers precisely; lines you resolve a conflict into entirely new content lose precise per-line attribution, but the original note is migrated wholesale to the new commit (preserving "this file had AI involvement"); if the AI resolves the conflict (a checkpoint ran during resolution) per-line attribution is recovered precisely
items.original: Copy semantics: the original commit's note is kept, not deleted
items.note: Requires the daemon; if git-ai cannot rebuild the old→new commit mapping it fails loudly and writes no attribution for that rebase (no silent fallback).

items.target: The new squash commit gets AI line attribution recomputed and written; prompt / session totals and custom attributes are preserved
items.original: The source branch's original commit SHAs and their notes are kept unchanged
items.note: Requires the daemon to observe the whole squash + commit sequence; a plain git squash merge is not attributed automatically.

items.target: Each merged-in original commit carries its own attribution into UAT, and blame still shows those lines as AI; the merge commit itself: a clean one-step merge carries no note; if you resolve conflicts and finish with git commit, it records attribution only for the conflict-resolution lines
items.original: The merged-in original commit SHAs and notes are unchanged
items.note: git-ai stats for the merge commit are deliberately skipped (performance), which does not mean attribution is lost.

items.target: The new commit created on UAT inherits the change's AI attribution (line numbers recomputed for the new position)
items.original: The source (dev) branch's original commit SHA and its note stay unchanged
items.note: Requires a live daemon; a plain git cherry-pick run without git-ai gets no automatic attribution.

items.target: The revert commit carries no attribution note by default and counts entirely as "unknown" in stats — the revert is not credited to AI
items.original: The reverted original commit and its AI attribution are kept unchanged
items.note: Reverting AI code does not "launder away" the original AI attribution.

attribution.propagationTitle: How attribution notes propagate (local by default)
attribution.propagation: Attribution lives in the repository's refs/notes/ai by default; plain git push / pull / clone does not carry it. With git-ai installed and the daemon live on the git-notes backend, push / pull / clone sync that ref automatically; otherwise run git ai fetch-notes manually. The optional HTTP backend keeps attribution entirely out of git — stored locally and uploaded to your own service. In short: attribution is machine/repo-local by default, and cross-machine sharing relies on git-ai's own sync rather than git's native note copying.

commands.hint: The commands below follow the official git-ai README and the calls this tool actually makes. Most actions are wrapped as buttons; this list is for cross-checking or troubleshooting in a terminal.

items.desc: Install / repair git-ai and write the checkpoint hook for supported AI agents (idempotent; agents already configured correctly are left untouched).
Only (re)write each agent's managed hook file, e.g. Claude's settings.json or the OpenCode plugin.
Print AI authorship stats for the current repository; add --json for a machine-readable structure; a commit range can be specified.
An AI-authorship-aware version of git blame, annotating each line with the agent / prompt that generated it. Supports all standard git blame flags.
Called automatically by each agent's edit hook to link every AI edit to a model / agent / prompt. Rarely run by hand; check whether it errors when troubleshooting.
A skill git-ai installs into ~/.agents/skills/: ask the agent that wrote the code about its instructions, decisions, and task intent. Use /ask directly inside your agent.

metrics.hint: Metric definitions share the same source as this tool's Stats / People pages (formulas.ts). total = human + unknown + AI, three buckets side by side.

items.q: hook reports "daemon startup blocked: lock held at ...daemon.lock"

a.text: The last git-ai daemon exited abnormally without releasing its file lock, so every hook command stays blocked. A stale-lock banner appears at the top of the Diagnostics page; follow it to delete daemon.lock and daemon.pid.json, then retry — the next git-ai command spins up a fresh daemon.

items.q: Metrics are all "Unattributed" / stats drop to zero

a.text: This means the AI agent's edits did not trigger the git-ai checkpoint hook. Open Diagnostics to see each agent's hook status and click "Fix this" for any that are missing; afterwards you must restart the agent and reopen the terminal.

items.q: Local blame shows AI annotations, but the remote team dashboard counts nothing

a.text: AI authorship lives in refs/notes/ai. git-ai's post-push hook normally syncs that ref to the remote after git push. If the team dashboard still sees nothing, sync the notes manually with these three steps:

a.caption: First fetch the remote AI notes into a temporary tracking ref.
Merge the remote AI notes into the local refs/notes/ai.
Push the merged AI notes back to the remote.

a.text: This syncs AI metadata (Git Notes), not code. Do not set git config --global remote.origin.push=refs/notes/* yourself — the wildcard also pushes git-ai's tracking ref refs/notes/ai-remote/*, affecting other repos and notes-based tools, contrary to git-ai practice.

branch.triggerTitle: Switch branch (a dirty tree blocks checkout)
branch.heading: Switch branch
branch.dirtyTitle: Working tree has {{n}} uncommitted changes; cannot switch to {{name}}
branch.dirtyDescription: Stage or commit on the Checkpoints page first, then retry

onboarding.title: Finish setup in three steps to see which lines your AI wrote
onboarding.description: Git AI Studio relies on the external git-ai CLI: install git-ai, add a repository to the Dashboard's aggregate set, and configure hooks for your AI agent before the Dashboard shows data.

checklist.repo: Add a repo to the Dashboard aggregate
checklist.hook: Configure a hook for an AI agent

deepLink.viewCheckpointsHint: Raw working_logs checkpoints for this HEAD

language.label: UI language
language.hint: Takes effect immediately; the choice is saved in local localStorage.

closeBehavior.title: When closing the window
closeBehavior.hint: With "Minimize to tray" selected, clicking the window's X won't quit the process; left-click the tray icon or choose "Show main window" from the tray menu to restore. The tray menu always provides a "Quit" entry.

autoLaunch.toastFailed: Failed to set launch at login
autoLaunch.title: Launch at login
autoLaunch.hint: When enabled, Git AI Studio starts automatically when you log in (pair it with "When closing the window → Minimize to tray" to keep it running in the background).
autoLaunch.subHint: The source of truth is the OS login item; turn this off before uninstalling the app to avoid leaving a stale startup entry.

ccSwitch.title: cc-switch guard
ccSwitch.hint: When cc-switch swaps a Codex profile it overwrites ~/.codex/config.toml wholesale, so the hooks section git-ai wrote is inevitably lost. When enabled, Studio watches that file for changes and, if the hook is missing, automatically runs git-ai install-hooks to restore it incrementally (toml_edit does a field-level merge and won't clash with the [model_providers.*] fields cc-switch writes).
ccSwitch.subHint: Off by default. Studio won't modify these files on your behalf without explicit permission.

lowAi.thresholdRangeError: Threshold must be an integer between 1 and 100
lowAi.realtimeHint: When off, it checks every 15 minutes; when on, it pushes within 1–3 seconds after a commit completes (the backend watches refs/notes/ai via fsnotify). Existing cooldowns (5 min after a repo switch / the configured remind interval) apply in both modes.
lowAi.autoEmailUnavailable: Not readable; will count the whole repo

daemon.toastSilenceReset: Daemon silence reset; you'll be reminded again the next time an issue is detected
daemon.title: git-ai daemon health alert
daemon.hint: When the background probe detects a daemon lock issue twice in a row, it alerts you via the OS notification center (macOS Notification Center / Linux libnotify / Windows toast).
daemon.subHint: When off, the 30s polling stops and no longer spawns tasklist subprocesses.

settings.pageSubtitle: App appearance, data storage, and key configuration entry points.

appearance.title: Appearance theme
appearance.systemHint: With "Follow system" selected, the app watches the OS light/dark preference and syncs automatically.

scanRoots.title: Scan roots (shared with the Repo page)

onboarding.title: First-run guide
onboarding.hint: Reopen the 5-step setup wizard: introduce Studio's capabilities, check the git-ai install, pick a repo, verify the hook, and wrap up. Uninstalling the client doesn't clear ~/.git-ai-studio/config.json, so the guide won't reappear by default after a reinstall — click here when you need it.

about.title: About
about.privacy: All data is processed locally and never uploaded.

repo.title: Repositories
repo.subtitle: Add the repos to roll up in the Dashboard's cross-repo view, or hit View to dig into one repo's Stats / People / Blame.

summary.aggregateHint: Added repos roll up into the Dashboard's cross-repo view.
summary.currentHint: Stats / People / Blame / the desktop pet all show this repo.

scanRoots.title: Scan roots
scanRoots.emptyHint: No directories added yet. Add the top-level folder where you keep your code, e.g.

all.title: All repositories
all.empty: No matching repositories. Check your scan roots or filter term.
all.emptyNoRoots: No repositories found yet. Expand "Scan roots & recently opened" below to add the top-level folder where you keep your code.

row.viewTooltip: Open this repo's single-repo views (Stats / People / Blame)

update.available: Update available: v{{version}} — click to view and install
update.installFailed: Failed to download or install the update

settings.title: Desktop pet
settings.hint: A little companion in the corner of your screen. A two-tone ring on its body reflects your live AI code share (purple = AI, blue = you), and it tints, shakes, and pops a message when the hook is missing, a commit fails to tag, the daemon gets stuck, and so on. Off by default, removable anytime, and it changes nothing else.
settings.themeHint: Switch the character; the data layer (purple = AI, blue = you) never changes.
settings.alertHint: On problems the whole pet tints, shakes, and pops a message; set the repeat interval.

states.hookMissing: Hook not configured — edits won't be attributed
